item.title: The journey to a datacenter in a box
item.description: It all started with silence. The hum of my old, decapitalized laptop, the one running my entire homelab and personal services, was gone. It had died. And the timing couldn’t have been worse. My personal services were down, my internal tools were offline, and I simply didn’t have the bandwidth to drop everything and spend a weekend performing a forensic recovery.

item.title: The Building Block Economy
item.description: The most effective way to build software and get massive adoption is no longer high quality mainline apps but via building blocks that enable and encourage others to build quantity over quality.1

item.title: Do not forget how you got here
item.description: This is a note to myself Don’t forget how you got here. During the whole of school you were essentially self taught. You did not pay any attention in class, yet you still managed to get through 13 years of school. You organised a rally trip from Madrid to Ulan Bator. You did this in a seat 127 that fell to pieces. No one believed it was possible, specially since the car would not even start 3 days before the trip was to start.

item.title: The sad state of smart homes
item.description: "A camel is a horse designed by committee." - Sir Alec Issigonis I am enamored by the idea of the smart home. Devices working to make life a little easier. Over the last few years, I've been adding smart home devices to our home, mainly for simple purposes like turning the porch lights or Chri

item.title: LambdaLemon
item.description: Now with 100% more gay!

item.title: Why Claude Mythos is not AGI
item.description: Claude Mythos Preview is not AGI.

item.title: Why I still build apps for myself
item.description: Why I still build small iOS apps for myself, even in an App Store where discovery is weak and indie development often feels irrational.

item.title: Verifying human authorship with human.json
item.description: Vouching for other people's websites with this brand new protocol that I thought would be fun to implement!

item.title: Full Text Search with IndexedDB
item.description: While working on Borogove there has been a desire to have full-text search of locally-stored chat message history. For web-based apps the main storage engine in use is IndexedDB, which is a rather low-level system and certainly doesn’t have easy facilities for this kind of operation. So what’s the simplest, performant way to implement a full-text search on IndexedDB?

item.title: I Tested Meta Muse Spark Against 4 Frontier Models
item.description: Three tests, five models, zero retries. Meta crushed vision and analysis but face-planted on code. ChatGPT wrote the worst code and produced the best output. Meta wins overall — barely.

item.title: Mindfulness, Programming, and the Mistake I Made
item.description: I tried to bring mindfulness into programming and thought I was failing. Measuring my attention revealed the real issue — I was using the wrong model for what mindfulness should feel like while coding.

item.title: Self-host your homelab tunnels on bunny.net
item.description: Principal software engineer with deep experience in full-stack TypeScript, custom web browsers and real-time, media-focused systems.

item.title: Claude Is Dead
item.description: In 1882 Nietzsche claimed that the dominant paradigm in Western society at the time, the existence of the Christian God and the guidelines for morality stemming from Christian tradition can no longer be justified. And went to shock the world (or at least the 7 people that used to read him at the time) and claimed that God is dead. So let me paraphrase him and try to be as flippant and outrageous as I can, and claim that the first modern god of the AI era is dead.

item.title: Overton
Porting Mac OS X to the Nintendo Wii
item.description: Mac OS X 10.0 (Cheetah) running natively on the Nintendo Wii

item.title: Škoda DuoBell: A bicycle bell that outsmarts even smart headphones - Škoda Storyboard
item.description: Pedestrians wearing headphones are exposed to an increased risk of accidents. In an effort to reduce collisions with cyclists, Škoda Auto, in collaboration with scientists, introduces an innovative bicycle bell whose sound can penetrate even active noise cancellation systems. In doing so, it helps prevent injuries to both pedestrians and cyclists.

item.title: I've sold out
item.description: I've sold out

item.title: Revision 2026 - Compo - PC Demo
item.description:
00:00:00 Intro 00:00:59 The Revenge of Amiga by Reality 00:07:22 Noctivagant by Copernicium 00:11:04 SQUAPHEREEN by ERNIE 00:13:22 Make πrogress not propaganda by [IAOTAI] CyReVolt 00:14:40 Hello Potato by 🥔 00:16:01 WaVeZzZ by lfo / TEAM VyRaL ^ Hokuto Force ^ RESiStANCE 00:17:12 REKINDLED by BrainWave 00:23:09 Korean Fish Demo by Royal Belgian Beer Squadron feat. Limp Ninja 00:26:59 trainn by Laso 00:27:54 trans rights are animal rights by Freja 00:29:33 IIX Bounces by mergeconflict 00:33:18 damocles by stargaze 00:36:50 Black Valley Flair by Skuggemannen & susencrusen 00:38:17 The ENTZ by PlayPsyCo & DESiRE 00:42:46 Tranceistor by Schranzvergleich 00:46:48 Voxelarium by Spectrals 00:51:21 Automata et Saltatio by Jumalauta 00:55:46 Love = Verb (Teaser) by Guideline 00:56:49 shaderland - swizzle's journey by canmom 00:59:39 On Demand by Suspend 01:03:20 ABOMINATION by MFX 01:08:21 HEART OF GLASS by GRAY MARCHERS & BYTERAPERS 01:14:41 Cerberus by Cocoon - Excess 01:22:50 Serpentin de Moire by Rebels and Calodox ( + guests) 01:26:08 Golden Egg by Gaspode 01:29:44 Every Beat Counts by Still 01:35:16 Razor1911
With commentary by our friends from SceneSat. https://scenesat.com/

item.title: The Git Commands I Run Before Reading Any Code
item.description: Five git commands that tell you where a codebase hurts before you open a single file. Churn hotspots, bus factor, bug clusters, and crisis patterns.

item.title: Multi-agentic Software Development is a Distributed Systems Problem (AGI can't save you from it)
item.description: Recently, I've been thinking a lot about scaffolding and languages for managing systems of LLMs coordinating with each other — new programming languages might be the ideal solution for this area.

item.title: The Hard Problems Nobody Has Solved - Log - nibzard
item.description: Four unsolved problems blocking the agentic future: correctness, architecture drift, context scaling, and judgment.

item.title: When Small Parquet Files Become a Big Problem (and How I Ended Up Writing a Compactor in PyArrow)
item.description: Learn how to compact small Parquet files using PyArrow with and without batching. A hands-on guide based on real NYC taxi data and a demo notebook.

item.title: Two Years of Valkey
item.description: Two years ago last month, a group of former contributors to the Redis project announced their intention to collaborate instead on a competitive fork. Triggered by the decision to shift Redis away from the permissive open source BSD license to source available alternatives – the Redis Source Available License (RSALv2) and Server Side Public License

item.title: The Life of a Claude Code Query
item.description: Production agentic systems are systems engineering artifacts. Classical OS patterns, including cache hierarchies, demand paging, process forking, and syscall interposition, re-emerge in AI agent design.

item.title: Ignore soft skills at your peril
item.description: Concentrating on your strengths makes you easy to manipulate.

item.title: The soul of an old machine
item.description: Michal Skalski's personal page

item.title: Vibe Coding Tools are a BattleMech · Alex Meub
item.description: Using modern AI coding tools feels like jumping into the cockpit of a BattleMech.

item.title: Engineering Is Moving Up the Stack
item.description: The debate about AI and software development is stuck on the wrong question. Most discussions ask whether AI writes good enough code, whether it matches human craftsmanship, whether it introduces bugs, whether developers can trust it. Reasonable questions. Increasingly beside the point. The shift that matters more isn’t about code quality. It’s about where engineering effort is required at all. AI is moving engineering up the stack, from writing code to designing systems to managing the lifecycle of software in production. The work doesn’t disappear. It shifts upward, toward broader and more structural concerns.

item.title: knoxnotes
item.description: 4.6.26 - I Sent This Blog From My Email App

item.title: Code is an afterthought
item.description: Currently working in the finance industry to humanize tools and make them more accessible to individuals. As a child, I enjoyed coding and creating games as a hobby.

item.title: NanoClaw's Architecture is a Masterclass in Doing Less
item.description: I pulled apart NanoClaw's 8,000-line codebase and found six architectural patterns that most SaaS teams should steal — credential proxies, container isola…
item.author: (John Gregoriadis)

item.title: For The Love of Internet
item.description: Two decades ago, when Indian ISPs had adequate IPv4 addresses, they were allocating free public IPs. I remember how exciting it was to host my own website from home and be able to access it from anywhere. Gaming was seamless with public IP. Peer to peer apps just worked. But as Internet users grew ISPs decided to deploy NAT. No more public IPs, no more free hosting. And it’s been like that for two decades now.

item.title: A Case for Mars (Dave Jarvis)
item.description: Software development blog

item.title: The issues with issue trackers
item.description: "I'd love to see that more unified." -- Linus Torvalds In this interview about Git, Linus Torvalds is asked what areas of the tool still need to evolve. He answ...

item.title: My now immaculate knowledge graph of life
item.description: Turning meeting transcripts into an Obsidian knowledge graph. Using Granola, a small Rust CLI, and a Claude skill, I extracted people and concepts from ~600 meetings and let the graph build itself. The result: a surprisingly dense map of relationships, ideas, and patterns hiding in everyday conversations.

item.title: What is a property?
item.description:
Posted on 2026-04-05
::
8 min read
:: Tags:
testing

item.title: Claude Is Not Your Architect. Stop Letting It Pretend.
item.description: Somewhere between 'ask Claude for a quick opinion' and 'Claude is writing our Jira tickets,' we lost the plot. AI agents are brilliant implementers. They're also confidently wrong about every decision that matters. And when it all falls over, they won't be the ones carrying the bag.

Bruges
This essay came together while I was thinking through interfaces, value, and surfaces in Bruges.

item.title: If you don't write it, I don't read it
item.description: Writing is sometimes repetitive, hard, and boring, but if you don't spend the time writing something, expect the same from your readers when they encounter your text.
